description.en: Return a new Struct object which writes and reads binary data according to the format string format. Creating a Struct object once and calling its methods is more efficient than calling module-level functions with the same format since the format string is only compiled once. Note The compiled versions of the most recent format strings passed to the module-level functions are cached, so programs that use only a few format strings needn’t worry about reusing a single Struct instance.
